facts.context: In humans, B is the allele for brown eyes and b is the allele for blue eyes. Two brothers both have brown eyes, but one of them has both the B and b alleles while the other only has B alleles. Which statement is true about the brothers?
facts.truth: They have the same phenotype but different genotypes.
distractors: They have the same genotype and phenotype.
They have different phenotypes and genotypes.
They have the same genotype but different phenotypes.

facts.context: Which unit of measurement should be used to describe the  mass of an apple?

In 1989, the unusually low snowpack in portions of the Sierra Nevada Mountains led to a widespread emergence of Edith's checkerspot butterflies in April. In these regions, flowers do not appear until May. What was the most likely result of the early emergence of these butterflies?
distractors: Most butterflies switched to other food sources.
Most butterflies adapted to the new environment.

facts.context: Which natural event would take the longest time to happen?
The length of a year is determined by the amount of time it takes
facts.truth: Earth to revolve around the Sun.
distractors: the Moon to revolve around Earth.
Earth to rotate on its axis.
the Sun to rotate on its axis.

Which of these best describes a fossil?
facts.truth: remains of an extinct organism found in a rock layer

facts.context: The boiling point of nitrogen is -196°C. What states of matter are involved when nitrogen boils?

A drug company developed a pill to reduce cases of stomach virus. To test the medication, half of the participants got the pill with the new medicine and half got a sugar pill. Which term best identifies the participants who got a sugar pill?
A company is making prototypes for a new computer system. Which of the following statements best describes an advantage of making several different prototypes?
facts.truth: They can be tested at the same time.
distractors: They can be produced in large numbers.
They will be bought by many different users.
They will be sold for more than they cost to build.

facts.context: A tree is a renewable resource because it can be
facts.truth: replaced with a newly planted tree.

facts.truth: be molded into almost any shape.

facts.context: A student must explain the role of dead leaves in the ecosystem. Which statement will the student most likely provide to explain the importance of these leaves?
facts.truth: The decay of the leaves provides nutrients to other plants.
distractors: The leaves provide shelter to growing seeds.
The leaves release chlorophyll into the grass as they decay.
The leaves prevent fungus from growing at the base of the tree.

facts.truth: a folded chain of amino acids
distractors: a branched chain of fatty acids
a branched chain of nucleic acids

facts.context: A student has recorded rainfall amounts for 28 days and uses a calculator to determine the total rainfall. What does the student calculate when she divides the total rainfall by 28?
When using a microscope, it is best to use a wet mount to
distractors: show the interior of sliced objects.
remove debris that is obscuring the object.

facts.context: In science, old ideas can be changed when new discoveries alter existing information. Which situation is most likely an example of a rethinking of an existing idea?
facts.truth: changing the definition of the term planet
distractors: discovering a new species of snake
developing an antibiotic to treat infections

facts.truth: take millions of years to form
